source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Analyses/Univariate/Bulletin17CAnalysis.cs @ fc28c0c -- A10 LOOSE smoke oracle. Bulletin17CAnalysis fits an LP3 model by GMM (default MultivariateNormal UQ) then computes the deterministic Cohn-style delta-method CIs (nested Gaussian quadrature over the GMM fit -- no MCMC/bootstrap seed dependence for the point_estimate/CI given the fitted parameters). The GMM fit uses BFGS + a numerical Jacobian (no RNG), so the parameters/point_estimates/CIs reproduce bit-identically across C++/R/Python. confidence_level and the exceedance-probability round-trip are exact structural invariants; point_estimate/lower_ci/upper_ci are SELF-COMPUTED at LOOSE rel tol; A11's emitter may tighten them against the real C# Bulletin17CAnalysis. The 25 default exceedance ordinates ascend, so the point-estimate curve descends.
